message:
fix(cass): Harden cass output parsing and add timeouts (bead 9xoi)

Problem:
- cassAvailable() could hang indefinitely if cass binary is unresponsive
- cassSearch() parsing was brittle when cass prints logs/warnings before
  JSON, returns NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON), or embeds JSON after a
  log prefix like "[INFO] ..."

Solution:
- Add 2-second timeout to cassAvailable() and cassNeedsIndex() to prevent
  hangs when the binary exists but is unresponsive
- Extract new parseCassJsonOutput() function with robust multi-strategy
  parsing:
  1. Try standard JSON.parse (happy path)
  2. Find first JSON-looking line and parse from there (leading logs)
  3. Parse line-by-line NDJSON, accumulating objects/arrays
  4. Scan for inline JSON values after log prefixes like "[INFO] ..."
- Apply new parser to cassSearch(), safeCassSearchWithDegraded(),
  cassStats(), and cassTimeline()
- Treat ENOENT quietly in cassAvailable() when quiet mode is on

Tests added:
- safeCassSearch parses hits when cass prints logs before JSON
- safeCassSearch parses NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
- safeCassSearch parses JSON when prefixed by inline log text
- cassTimeline tolerates leading logs before JSON
- safeCassSearch(force) parses output even when cass exits non-zero
- safeCassSearch(force) parses NDJSON even when cass exits non-zero

This makes cass integration resilient to version differences, debug
output, and various output formats the cass binary might produce.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-69v","title":"[Foundation] package.json with exact dependency versions","description":"Create package.json with exact dependency versions and build configuration for the CASS Memory System CLI tool.

## Context
The CASS Memory System requires a well-defined package.json that supports cross-platform binary compilation, ESM modules, and integration with multiple LLM providers through the Vercel AI SDK.

## Technical Specification

### Dependencies:
- ai@^4.0.0 - Vercel AI SDK core for provider-agnostic LLM integration
- @ai-sdk/openai@^1.0.0 - OpenAI provider (GPT-4, GPT-3.5)
- @ai-sdk/anthropic@^1.0.0 - Anthropic provider (Claude models)
- @ai-sdk/google@^1.0.0 - Google provider (Gemini models)
- zod@^3.23.0 - Runtime type validation for structured LLM outputs
- yaml@^2.3.0 - YAML parsing for playbook and config files
- chalk@^5.3.0 - Terminal output styling for user-friendly CLI

### DevDependencies:
- @types/bun@latest - TypeScript types for Bun runtime
- typescript@^5.0.0 - TypeScript compiler for type checking

### Scripts:
- dev: bun run src/cass-memory.ts - Development mode with hot reload
- build: bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --outfile dist/cass-memory - Build for current platform
- build:all: Build for linux, macos, and windows
- build:linux: bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=linux-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-linux
- build:macos: bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=darwin-arm64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-macos
- build:windows: bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=windows-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-windows.exe
- typecheck: tsc --noEmit - Type checking without compilation
- test: bun test - Run test suite

### Configuration:
- type: module - Enable ES modules
- bin: cass-memory -> ./dist/cass-memory.js - CLI entry point
- name: cass-memory
- version: 0.1.0 - Initial version
- private: true - Not published to npm (distributed as binary)

## Reasoning

### Why Vercel AI SDK:
1. Provider Agnostic: Single interface for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google - users choose their preferred LLM
2. Structured Output: Native Zod integration for reliable structured responses (critical for playbook rule extraction)
3. Streaming Support: Real-time output for long-running operations
4. TypeScript-First: Excellent type safety and IntelliSense
5. Active Development: Well-maintained with regular updates

### Why Bun:
1. Single Binary: Bun can compile TypeScript to standalone executables
2. Fast: Native speed for file operations and process management
3. Built-in TypeScript: No transpilation needed during development
4. Cross-platform: Compile once, run anywhere

### Why ESM:
1. Modern Standard: ES modules are the future of JavaScript
2. Better Tree Shaking: Smaller bundle sizes
3. AI SDK Requirement: Vercel AI SDK v4 requires ESM

## Acceptance Criteria

- package.json exists in project root
- All dependencies listed with correct versions
- All devDependencies listed
- All build scripts present and functional
- type: module is set
- bin entry points to correct file
- bun install works without errors
- bun run dev starts development mode
- bun run build creates executable binary
- bun run typecheck passes with no errors

## How This Serves the Universal Agent Memory System

The package.json is the foundation contract for the entire CASS Memory System. It ensures:

1. Multi-Provider Support: Agents can use any LLM provider their organization prefers
2. Reliable Distribution: Compiled binaries mean no dependency hell for end users
3. Type Safety: Zod validation ensures LLM outputs conform to expected schemas
4. Developer Experience: Clear scripts and tooling enable rapid iteration
5. Cross-Platform: Agents on any OS can use CASS Memory System

Without proper dependency management, the system cannot reliably integrate with LLMs or provide consistent behavior across environments.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:21.842607-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:39.80838-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:36.645512-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-6jdf","title":"[Foundation] Define .cass/ repo-level directory structure","description":"Define and implement the .cass/ repository-level directory structure for project-specific CASS Memory System data.

## Context
The .cass/ directory contains project-specific memory that is committed to version control and shared with the entire team. This enables 'memory as code' - the team's collective knowledge about the project is versioned alongside the codebase.

## Technical Specification

### Directory Structure:
.cass/
├── playbook.yaml            # Project-specific playbook (shared with team)
├── config.yaml              # Project-level configuration overrides
└── toxic.log                # Project-level blocked patterns

### File Specifications:

#### playbook.yaml (Project Playbook):
- Project-specific rules that apply to this repository only
- Merged with user's global playbook (~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml)
- Project rules take precedence over global rules
- Categories: PROJECT_SETUP, BUILD, TEST, DEPLOY, ARCHITECTURE, etc.
- Each bullet has:
  - content: The rule itself
  - context: When/why this rule applies
  - priority: 0-10 (higher = more important)
  - state: PROVEN, TESTING, CANDIDATE
  - created_at: ISO timestamp
  - created_by: User/agent who added it
  - tags: [backend, frontend, ci, etc.]

Example:
yaml
bullets:
  - content: Always run tests with --coverage flag
    context: Team requires 80% coverage for all PRs
    priority: 9
    state: PROVEN
    created_at: 2025-12-01T10:00:00Z
    created_by: cass-memory
    tags: [test, ci]
  
  - content: Use Bun instead of npm for package management
    context: Project uses Bun for faster installs and built-in TypeScript support
    priority: 10
    state: PROVEN
    created_at: 2025-12-01T10:00:00Z
    created_by: cass-memory
    tags: [setup, deps]


#### config.yaml (Project Configuration):
- Overrides for project-specific settings
- Does NOT contain API keys (those stay in ~/.cass-memory/config.json)
- Can specify:
  - preferred_model: Override user's default model for this project
  - enable_curation: Project-level curation toggle
  - evidence_threshold: Custom threshold for this project
  - excluded_paths: Paths to ignore in reflections (e.g., generated/, vendor/)
  - custom_categories: Project-specific bullet categories

Example:
yaml
preferred_model: gpt-4  # This project uses GPT-4 for complex reasoning
enable_curation: true
evidence_threshold: 5
excluded_paths:
  - generated/
  - vendor/
  - node_modules/
custom_categories:
  - ARCHITECTURE
  - API_DESIGN
  - DATABASE


#### toxic.log (Project Blocklist):
- Newline-delimited list of blocked bullet content
- Project-specific patterns to block
- Committed to git so entire team blocks same patterns
- Case-insensitive matching
- Merged with user's global toxic_bullets.log

Example:
text
Always use var for variables
Disable all linting rules
Skip writing tests for this file


## Reasoning

### Why .cass/ (Hidden Directory):
1. Unix Convention: Hidden directories for metadata
2. Doesn't Clutter: Not visible in normal directory listings
3. Tool Convention: Like .git/, .github/, .vscode/

### Why playbook.yaml in Git:
1. Memory as Code: Team knowledge is versioned
2. Shared Learning: New team members inherit project wisdom
3. Code Review: Changes to playbook go through PR review
4. Reproducibility: Same rules apply to all developers
5. Time Travel: git log shows how project knowledge evolved

### Why config.yaml Separate from config.json:
1. Security: config.json (global) has API keys, never committed
2. Team Settings: config.yaml (repo) has project settings, always committed
3. Overrides: Repo settings override user settings

### Why toxic.log in Git:
1. Team Safety: Everyone blocks the same bad patterns
2. Onboarding: New devs see what NOT to do
3. Historical Record: Track why certain patterns were blocked
4. Consistency: Entire team learns from mistakes

### Why Project Playbook Takes Precedence:
1. Specificity: Project needs override general preferences
2. Team Alignment: Everyone follows project standards
3. Flexibility: Users keep their personal rules for other projects

## Acceptance Criteria

- .cass/ directory is created on cass-memory init (repo mode)
- playbook.yaml created with empty bullets array
- config.yaml created with sensible defaults (or omitted if no overrides)
- toxic.log created as empty file
- .cass/ directory is NOT in .gitignore (should be committed)
- Helper functions in utils.ts: resolveRepoDir(), ensureRepoStructure()
- Playbook loading merges global + repo playbooks (repo takes precedence)
- Config loading merges global + repo configs (repo takes precedence)
- Toxic patterns merged from both global and repo logs
- cass-memory doctor verifies .cass/ structure

## How This Serves the Universal Agent Memory System

The .cass/ directory enables team-level memory sharing:

1. Memory as Code: Team knowledge is versioned alongside code
2. Collective Learning: Entire team benefits from lessons learned
3. Onboarding: New team members inherit project wisdom immediately
4. Consistency: All agents follow same project-specific rules
5. Code Review: Memory changes go through same review process as code
6. Time Travel: git history shows evolution of team knowledge
7. Multi-Agent Coordination: Multiple agents share same memory layer

This is the key innovation that makes CASS Memory a universal system - memory persists not just per user, but per project, and is versioned in git like any other critical project artifact.

Without repo-level playbooks, teams would constantly reinvent the wheel, onboarding would be slow, and agents would have inconsistent behavior across team members.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:57.98691-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:21:09.19667-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:21:03.05494-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-6ob","title":"[Playbook] findSimilarBullet - Find most similar existing bullet","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function findSimilarBullet(
  bullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  content: string,
  threshold: number
): { found: boolean; bullet?: PlaybookBullet; similarity?: number }
```

## Purpose
Find the most similar existing bullet above a threshold. Returns similarity score for logging and decision-making.

## Algorithm
1. Filter out deprecated bullets
2. Calculate similarity for ALL non-deprecated bullets
3. Find bullet with highest similarity
4. If highest >= threshold:
   - Return { found: true, bullet, similarity }
5. Else:
   - Return { found: false }

## Difference from isDuplicateBullet
- **isDuplicateBullet**: Returns first match, boolean check, prevents duplicates
- **findSimilarBullet**: Returns highest match, includes score, suggests similar

## Return Value Usage
```typescript
const result = findSimilarBullet(bullets, newContent, 0.7);
if (result.found) {
  console.log(`Found similar bullet (${result.similarity.toFixed(2)}): ${result.bullet.content}`);
  // Ask user: merge, replace, or add anyway?
}
```

## Threshold Selection
- **High threshold (0.85+)**: Only very similar bullets
- **Medium threshold (0.70-0.85)**: Suggest as related
- **Low threshold (0.50-0.70)**: Broad similarity search

## Implementation Notes
- Must search ALL bullets to find highest
- Skip deprecated bullets (outdated)
- Include similarity score in result
- Useful for merge suggestions

## Usage Scenarios
1. **Interactive add**: Show similar bullets to user
2. **Merge suggestions**: Find candidates for combining
3. **Search**: Find bullets related to query
4. **Analytics**: Measure playbook clustering

## Dependencies
- jaccardSimilarity() utility function

## Related Functions
- isDuplicateBullet() - simpler boolean check
- findMergeCandidates() - finds multiple similar groups
- getBulletsByCategory() - category-based filtering","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.773781-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:02:06.300218-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:01:55.424422-05:00"}
+{"id":"cass_memory_system-6r0k","title":"[Bug][curation] Avoid flakey inversion near prune threshold (decayed float drift)","description":"# Problem
`curatePlaybook()` uses **decayed** helpful/harmful counts (floating point) and compares them to integer thresholds (e.g. `pruneHarmfulThreshold = 3`).

Because feedback timestamps and `now` differ by milliseconds/seconds, each “recent” event can decay to slightly under `1.0` (e.g. `0.9999999`). This makes sums like `3 events` become `2.9999997`, causing strict `>= 3` comparisons to fail unpredictably.

## Why it matters
- Inversion/deprecation behavior can be non-deterministic near thresholds (especially in tests and rapid feedback loops).
- A rule marked harmful N times “right now” should behave like N, not N-ε.

## Fix
- Use a small epsilon in threshold comparisons where we treat integer thresholds as “count-like”.
- Add a deterministic regression test that uses timestamps slightly in the past (e.g. 1s old) so the bug would reproduce without the epsilon.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inversion triggers at the configured prune threshold even with slight decay (covered by test).
- [ ] No behavior change for genuinely stale/decayed events.
- [ ] `bun test` + `bun run typecheck` pass.

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-6syf","title":"[Critical] Fix types.ts to match scoring.ts and cass.ts requirements","description":"","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-07T18:47:34.297635-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:53:18.24423-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:14.675127-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-6u3","title":"[Utils] truncateForContext - Large content truncation for LLM","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:16.512693-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:48:01.792191-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:46:53.434325-05:00","comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-6u3","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function truncateForContext(text: string, maxChars: number): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Check if text.length <= maxChars
- If yes, return text unchanged
- If no, truncate and append informative message:
  - text.slice(0, maxChars) + "\n\n[... truncated for context limit ...]"
- Use \n\n for clear separation
- Message explains WHY truncated (not just "...")

## Why This Matters
Used before sending session content to LLM to avoid token limits. Typical maxChars might be 100,000 (roughly 25k tokens). Clear message helps LLM understand context was truncated.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Very small maxChars - ensure message fits or adjust
- Empty string - return empty
- Exactly at limit - return unchanged
- Multiple sessions concatenated - truncate whole string, not per session
- Preserve important content at start (don't truncate from middle)","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-70sd","title":"[Unit Tests] config.ts - Configuration loading and merging","description":"## Unit Tests: config.ts - Configuration Loading and Merging

### Test Categories

#### 1. loadGlobalConfig()
- Returns default config when ~/.cass/config.yaml missing
- Parses valid YAML with all fields
- Handles partial configs (missing optional fields)
- Logs warning for malformed YAML but returns defaults

#### 2. loadRepoConfig()
- Returns null when .cass/config.yaml missing
- Parses valid repo config
- Respects working directory parameter
- Handles nested playbook_paths array

#### 3. mergeConfigs()
- Global config provides base values
- Repo config overrides global values
- Nested objects merge correctly (llm settings)
- Arrays concatenate (playbook_paths)
- Explicit nulls in repo override global values
- Empty repo config returns global unchanged

#### 4. findRepoRoot()
- Finds .cass directory in current path
- Walks up directory tree to find .cass
- Returns undefined at filesystem root
- Handles symlinked directories

#### 5. Edge Cases
- Unicode content in YAML files
- Very large config files
- Read-only file permissions
- Config with unknown keys (forward compatibility)

### Test Data Requirements
- Sample config YAML files in test/fixtures/configs/
- Both valid and malformed examples
- Configs with various merge scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:23:23.219085-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T22:19:23.813898-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T22:19:23.813898-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-70sd","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-yj1z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:04.23662-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-70sd","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-p4mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:04.369118-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-70sd","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-ep5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:07.779792-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-9stx","title":"[Unit] scoring.ts - Decay Algorithm & Score Computation","description":"## Target: src/scoring.ts (157 LOC) - ZERO CURRENT COVERAGE

### Functions to Test:

1. **computeDecayedScore(feedbackEvents, options)** - Core decay algorithm
   - Single helpful event → positive score
   - Single harmful event → negative score
   - Multiple events → aggregated score
   - Old events decay more than recent events
   - 90-day half-life verification
   - Empty events array → 0 or baseline
   - Events at exact half-life boundary
   - Events from future (edge case)

2. **calculateHalfLife(event, baseHalfLife)** - Half-life calculation
   - Default 90-day half-life
   - Custom half-life values
   - Boundary conditions

3. **scoreWithContext(bullet, queryContext)** - Contextual scoring
   - Scope match bonus
   - Keyword overlap bonus
   - Recency bonus
   - Combined factors
   - Missing context gracefully handled

4. **rankBullets(bullets, query, options)** - Bullet ranking
   - Sort by score descending
   - Ties broken consistently
   - Empty array → empty array
   - Single bullet → single result
   - Limit parameter respected

5. **normalizeScores(bullets)** - Score normalization
   - Scores in 0-1 range
   - Max score becomes 1
   - Min score becomes 0
   - All same scores → all equal

### Mathematical Properties to Verify:
- Decay is exponential, not linear
- Score(t) = Score(0) * 0.5^(t/halfLife)
- Helpful and harmful don't simply cancel
- Temporal ordering affects result

### Test Data Patterns:
- Use fixed timestamps, not Date.now()
- Create event generators for various scenarios
- Verify floating-point edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:27:47.579668-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:57:20.803278-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:57:20.803278-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9stx","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-uufj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:15.323057-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9stx","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-mmxv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:58.922273-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-9tv6","title":"[UX][output] Polish reflect UX (progress + dry-run output)","description":"Problem
- `cm reflect` is a long-running operation that benefits from a premium progress narrative (what it’s doing, how many sessions, what changed).
- `--dry-run` currently prints raw JSON even in human mode, which is jarring and hard to read.

What to do
- Add clear progress reporting for reflect (without spamming):
  - sessions discovered / processed
  - per-scope summary (global/repo)
  - error summary + next steps
- Improve `--dry-run` human output: render deltas using the output design system (with a JSON option preserved).

Constraints
- Keep `--json` output stable and machine-readable.","design":"Implementation sketch
- Use output helpers for sections + lists + truncation.
- If deltas are large, show a summary plus “use --json for full detail”.","acceptance_criteria":"- `cm reflect` shows clear progress and a clean final summary.
- `cm reflect --dry-run` in human mode prints a readable delta preview (not raw JSON).
- `cm reflect --dry-run --json` remains pure JSON.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:32:50.954879-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T19:12:20.081275-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T19:12:20.081275-05:00","labels":["output","reflect","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9tv6","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-3tds","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:32:55.471289-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9tv6","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-r1hq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:32:55.515022-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-9ui","title":"[Cass] cassAvailable - Check if cass is installed and working","description":"Implement cassAvailable() to check if cass CLI is installed and functional.

## Signature
```typescript
function cassAvailable(cassPath = "cass"): boolean
```

## CLI Command
`cass health`

## Implementation
- Use execSync() with 200ms timeout
- Set stdio: "pipe" to suppress output
- Return true if exit code 0
- Return false on any error (timeout, non-zero exit, not found)
- Catch and suppress all exceptions

## Error Handling
- No exceptions thrown - always returns boolean
- Logs error at debug level only
- Safe to call frequently without performance impact

## Usage
Called before any cass operation to fail fast:
```typescript
if (!cassAvailable()) {
  console.error("cass CLI not found");
  return null;
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: cass is healthy and available
- Any other: cass unavailable or not working","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:53.647353-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:53:18.248467-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:49:54.115493-05:00"}
+{"id":"cass_memory_system-9xoi","title":"[Bug][reliability] Stabilize CLI robustness (cass parsing, warn(), no process.exit)","description":"# Context

We have a few CLI-facing reliability footguns that can cause hangs, confusing output parsing, or hard process exits when these modules are used programmatically (e.g., MCP server / tests).

## Problems
- `cassAvailable()` can hang indefinitely if the `cass` binary is present but unresponsive.
- `cassSearch()` parsing is brittle when `cass` prints logs/warnings before JSON, returns NDJSON, or embeds JSON after a log prefix (e.g., `[INFO] ...`).
- LLM/key validation and retry warnings were using raw `console.warn`, producing inconsistent formatting and making tests brittle.
- Some command modules call `process.exit(1)` on validation errors, which is hostile to programmatic callers/tests.

## Goals
- Never hang on `cass --version`.
- Robustly parse `cass` output across: JSON array/object, leading logs, NDJSON, and inline log prefixes.
- Centralize warning output through the shared logger helper (`warn()`), keeping consistent CLI prefixing.
- Prefer throwing errors over `process.exit()` inside command modules.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cassAvailable()` uses a short timeout and treats ENOENT quietly.
- [ ] `cassSearch()` parsing handles: leading log lines + JSON, NDJSON, and log-prefix + JSON (covered by tests).
- [ ] LLM warnings/retry logs go through `warn()` (not raw `console.warn`).
- [ ] `auditCommand()` / `validateCommand()` throw instead of exiting.
- [ ] `bun run typecheck` and `bun test` pass.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T22:09:39.098905-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T22:16:37.440297-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T22:16:37.440297-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-a0k1","title":"[CLI:playbook] stats - Playbook statistics","description":"Display playbook health and statistics.

Command signature:
```bash
cass-memory playbook stats [--json]
```

Implementation:
1. Load playbook
2. Calculate statistics:
   - Total bullets by scope (global/workspace)
   - Distribution by maturity
   - Distribution by category
   - Top performers (highest scores)
   - At-risk bullets (low scores, old age)
   - Deprecated count
   - Time-based metrics

Output format:
```
PLAYBOOK STATISTICS

Overview:
  Total bullets: 42
  Global: 35
  Workspace-scoped: 7
  Deprecated: 3

Maturity Distribution:
  Proven: 5 (12%)
  Accepted: 18 (43%)
  Candidates: 19 (45%)

Category Distribution:
  best-practice: 15
  tools: 12
  workflow: 8
  pitfall: 5
  patterns: 2

Top Performers (highest scores):
  1. b-abc123 (0.95) - Always run tests before committing
  2. b-def456 (0.92) - Use TypeScript strict mode
  3. b-ghi789 (0.88) - Prefer composition over inheritance

At Risk (need attention):
  1. b-jkl012 (0.32, 120 days old) - Use jQuery for DOM manipulation
     → Low score, may need deprecation
  2. b-mno345 (0.41, 90 days old) - Avoid async/await
     → Contradicted by recent evidence

Health Score: 78/100
  ✓ Good maturity distribution
  ✓ Active curation (last update: 2 days ago)
  ⚠ 2 bullets at risk
  ✓ No duplicate content detected
```

Statistics calculated:
- Maturity distribution (percentages)
- Category breakdown
- Top 5 highest scores
- Bottom 5 scores with age (at-risk)
- Overall health score (0-100)
- Last update timestamp
- Duplicates detection (similar content)

Health score factors:
- Maturity pyramid (more proven than candidates = good)
- Recent updates (active curation)
- Low at-risk count
- No duplicates
- Balanced category distribution

This provides quick playbook health check and actionable insights.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:56.170391-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:58:47.058657-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:57:54.378615-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-a2y3","title":"[Semantic] findSemanticDuplicates - Embedding-based dedup","description":"Detect semantic duplicates for merge candidates.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function findSemanticDuplicates(
  bullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  threshold: number = 0.85
): Promise<{ pair: [string, string]; similarity: number }[]>
```

Process:
1. Ensure all bullets have embeddings
2. Compare all pairs (O(n²) but n is small)
3. For each pair:
   - Calculate cosine similarity
   - If > threshold, record as duplicate
4. Return pairs sorted by similarity

Use cases:
- Stats command: Show merge candidates
- Playbook cleanup: Identify duplicates
- Quality assurance: Find redundant rules

Example output:
```typescript
[
  {
    pair: ["b-123", "b-456"],
    similarity: 0.94,
    bullets: [
      "Use async/await for all DB calls",
      "Always use async/await when calling database"
    ]
  },
  {
    pair: ["b-789", "b-012"],
    similarity: 0.87,
    bullets: [
      "Mock external APIs in tests",
      "Use mocks for third-party services in unit tests"
    ]
  }
]
```

Threshold selection:
- 0.95+: Almost identical (definite merge)
- 0.85-0.95: Very similar (probable merge)
- 0.75-0.85: Similar (review for merge)
- <0.75: Different enough to keep

Performance:
- 100 bullets: ~500ms (4950 comparisons)
- 500 bullets: ~12s (124750 comparisons)
- Could optimize with clustering (future)

Presented in stats command as:

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-a3y4","title":"[Semantic] EmbeddingCache type - Cached embeddings storage","description":"Cache structure for storing pre-computed embeddings.

File: ~/.cass-memory/embeddings/bullets.json

Structure:
```typescript
interface EmbeddingCache {
  version: string;           // Cache format version
  model: string;             // Model used (for invalidation)
  bullets: Record<string, {
    contentHash: string;     // Hash of content when computed
    embedding: number[];     // 384-dimensional vector
    computedAt: string;      // ISO timestamp
  }>;
}
```

Invalidation logic:
- If content hash changed → recompute
- If model changed → recompute all
- If cache version changed → rebuild cache

Cache benefits:
- Embeddings computed once
- Startup time: ~50ms vs ~300ms
- Model stays loaded in memory

Cache size:
- 100 bullets × 384 dims × 8 bytes = ~300KB
- Negligible disk space
- Fast to load/save

Example:
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "model": "Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
  "bullets": {
    "b-abc123": {
      "contentHash": "sha256:...",
      "embedding": [0.123, -0.456, ...],
      "computedAt": "2025-12-07T10:00:00Z"
    }
  }
}
```","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacHill","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:32.970005-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T04:31:43.555698-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T04:31:43.555698-05:00","comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-a3y4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Cache structure for storing pre-computed embeddings.

  }>;
}
```

Invalidation logic:
- If content hash changed → recompute
- If model changed → recompute all
- If cache version changed → rebuild cache

Cache benefits:
- Embeddings computed once
- Startup time: ~50ms vs ~300ms
- Model stays loaded in memory

Cache size:
- 100 bullets × 384 dims × 8 bytes = ~300KB
- Negligible disk space
- Fast to load/save

Example:
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "model": "Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
  "bullets": {

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-d06","title":"[Security] Sanitization config schema","description":"Define the sanitization configuration schema in config.json.

Schema:
```typescript
sanitization: {
  enabled: boolean;           // Global kill switch for sanitization
  extraPatterns: string[];    // Additional regex patterns (user-defined)
}
```

Example config.json:
```json
{
  "sanitization": {
    "enabled": true,
    "extraPatterns": [
      "INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY",
      "MY_COMPANY_SECRET_\\w+",
      "ACME_CORP_[A-Za-z0-9]{16}"
    ]
  }
}
```

Purpose:
- Allow users to add company-specific or project-specific patterns
- Provide global on/off switch (enabled flag)
- Patterns are strings that will be compiled to RegExp with 'gi' flags

Default config (if not specified):
```json
{
  "sanitization": {
    "enabled": true,
    "extraPatterns": []
  }
}
```

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-d1r","title":"[Diary] listRecentDiaries - Get diaries by age","description":"List diary entries modified within specified number of days.

Function Signature:
async function listRecentDiaries(
  days: number,
  config: Config
): Promise<DiaryEntry[]>

Process:
1. Calculate cutoff date: now - days
2. List all files in diary directory
3. Filter for JSON files matching diary filename pattern
4. Check file modification time (mtime)
5. Keep only files modified after cutoff
6. Load each matching diary using loadDiaryEntry
7. Sort by diary.timestamp descending (newest first)
8. Return sorted array

Sorting:
- Primary: timestamp descending (newest first)
- Secondary: agent alphabetically (for same timestamp)

Error Handling:
- Skip corrupted/unreadable diaries
- Log warnings for skipped files
- Never fail entire operation due to single bad file

Key Features:
- Time-based filtering for recency
- Sorted results for consistency
- Resilient to corrupted entries
- Efficient file stat usage

Use Cases:
- Stats command: 'cass stats --days 7'
- Recent activity summary
- Trend analysis over time windows
- Dashboard recent entries display

Example Usage:
// Get last week's diaries
const recentDiaries = await listRecentDiaries(7, config);
console.log();

// Get today's diaries
const todayDiaries = await listRecentDiaries(1, config);

Performance:
- O(n log n) due to sorting where n = matching diaries
- File stat is fast (no content reading until filter passes)
- Consider pagination for very large results

Output:
Returns array of DiaryEntry objects sorted newest first.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:37.928906-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:13:46.19546-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:13:39.476994-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-d6yl","title":"Enhance Doctor: Auto-fix and self-test","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-08T00:16:37.173901-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T00:16:45.125599-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T00:16:45.125599-05:00"}
+{"id":"cass_memory_system-da6p","title":"[Bug][reliability] Ensure all cass invocations are timeout-safe + parse logs","description":"# Problem
Some cass helper functions still call `spawnSync`/`JSON.parse` directly without a timeout or log-tolerant parsing. If the `cass` binary is present but wedged (or emits log prefixes), these codepaths can hang or break in surprising ways.

## Current risky spots
- `cassNeedsIndex()` uses `spawnSync(..., ["health"])` without a timeout.
- `safeCassSearchWithDegraded(..., { force: true })` fallback uses `spawnSync` without a timeout and does a raw `JSON.parse(text)` (ignores leading logs/NDJSON/inline prefix).
- `cassStats()` / `cassTimeline()` do raw `JSON.parse(stdout)` even though CLIs sometimes emit log lines before JSON.

## Fix
- Add timeouts to remaining `spawnSync` calls (health + force fallback).
- Reuse the robust cass JSON parsing helper for the `force` fallback and for stats/timeline parsing.
- Add regression tests covering:
  - `cassTimeline()` parsing when logs precede JSON
  - `safeCassSearch(..., { force: true })` parsing when logs/NDJSON appear

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `spawnSync` cass calls without a timeout.
- [ ] Stats/timeline parsing tolerates leading logs.
- [ ] Force fallback parsing tolerates leading logs + NDJSON.
- [ ] `bun run typecheck` and `bun test` pass.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T23:43:11.044465-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T23:46:51.856432-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T23:46:51.856432-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-dke","title":"[Playbook] isDuplicateBullet - Semantic duplicate detection","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function isDuplicateBullet(
  newContent: string,
  existingBullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  threshold: number = 0.85
): { isDuplicate: boolean; matchingBullet?: PlaybookBullet }
```

## Purpose
Detect semantic duplicates before adding new bullets. Prevents near-identical rules from cluttering the playbook.

## Algorithm
1. Filter out deprecated bullets (state === "retired")
2. For each active bullet:
   - Calculate jaccardSimilarity(newContent, bullet.content)
   - If similarity >= threshold: return match
3. If no match found: return { isDuplicate: false }

## Jaccard Similarity
```typescript
jaccard(A, B) = |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B|
```
- Tokenize both strings (split on whitespace/punctuation)
- Calculate intersection size
- Calculate union size
- Return intersection / union

Range: 0.0 (completely different) to 1.0 (identical)

## Threshold Guidelines
- **0.85 (default)**: Very similar, likely duplicate
- **0.75**: Moderately similar, manual review needed
- **0.90**: Almost identical, definitely duplicate

## Example Duplicates (similarity > 0.85)
- "Always run tests before committing"
- "Run all tests before you commit"
→ Same meaning, different wording

## Implementation Notes
- Case-insensitive comparison
- Ignore punctuation differences
- Skip deprecated bullets (may be outdated versions)
- Return first match (don't search all)

## Usage Scenarios
1. **Curator**: Check before adding extracted rule
2. **Manual add**: Warn user if duplicate exists
3. **Merge detection**: Find similar rules to combine

## Dependencies
- jaccardSimilarity() utility function

## Related Functions
- findSimilarBullet() - returns similarity score
- findMergeCandidates() - uses similarity for grouping
- addBullet() - should check duplicates first","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:59.274031-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:26:00.896916-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:25:12.11436-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-dofz","title":"[Critical] Simplified Diary Schema for V1","description":"# Simplified Diary Schema for V1

## The Problem
Current DiaryEntry schema has 15+ fields including:
- decisions, problems, solutions, tools, patterns
- filesModified, filesByImpact, dependencies
- architectureChanges, codeQuality, testingApproach
- performanceConsiderations, securityNotes, technicalDebt

This is MASSIVELY over-engineered for v1. 
Extracting all this requires multiple LLM calls and complex prompts.
Most fields will be empty most of the time.

## The Solution
Start with a minimal diary schema that covers 80% of value:

### V1 Diary Schema (Minimal):
```typescript
interface DiaryEntryV1 {
  id: string;
  sessionPath: string;
  timestamp: Date;
  
  // Core fields only
  summary: string;         // What happened in 1-2 sentences
  outcome: "success" | "partial" | "failure" | "unknown";
  
  // Optional simple fields
  task?: string;           // What was the user trying to do
  keyInsight?: string;     // One key learning (if any)
  filesChanged?: string[]; // Just the paths
  
  // Metadata
  duration?: number;       // Seconds
  agent: string;           // Which agent
}
```

### Comparison:
| V1 (Minimal) | Current Plan (Full) |
|--------------|---------------------|
| 7 fields | 18+ fields |
| 1 LLM call | 3-5 LLM calls |
| Simple extraction | Complex multi-step |
| Fast (~2s) | Slow (~10s) |
| Works local-only | Requires LLM |

### V1 Extraction (No LLM Required):
```typescript
async function extractDiaryV1(sessionPath: string): Promise<DiaryEntryV1> {
  const session = await loadSession(sessionPath);
  
  return {
    id: generateId(),
    sessionPath,
    timestamp: session.startTime,
    
    // Extract from session metadata (no LLM)
    summary: session.title || "Coding session",
    outcome: inferOutcome(session),  // From exit code, errors
    task: session.userMessages[0]?.slice(0, 200),  // First user message
    filesChanged: extractFilePaths(session),
    duration: session.duration,
    agent: session.agent
  };
}

function inferOutcome(session: Session): Outcome {
  if (session.exitCode === 0 && !session.hasErrors) return "success";
  if (session.exitCode !== 0) return "failure";
  if (session.hasErrors) return "partial";
  return "unknown";
}
```

### Upgrade Path to Full Schema:
```typescript
// V2 adds more fields (with LLM)
interface DiaryEntryV2 extends DiaryEntryV1 {
  decisions?: string[];
  patterns?: string[];
  problems?: string[];
}

// V3 adds rich fields
interface DiaryEntryV3 extends DiaryEntryV2 {
  architectureChanges?: ArchChange[];
  testingApproach?: TestApproach;
  // ... etc
}

// Migration is additive - old entries still valid
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Simpler = faster to implement
- Simpler = less LLM cost
- Simpler = works offline
- Simpler = fewer bugs
- Can always add fields later (additive)
- Full schema is over-engineering for v1","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:07.65785-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T00:51:44.566781-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T00:51:44.566781-05:00"}
